data.title: Ocugen outlines 2025 milestones for gene therapy programs with BLA filings on track
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ocugen (OCGN) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Shankar Musunuri highlighted advancements in Ocugen’s modifier gene therapy platform, specifically targeting three biologics license application (BLA) filings over the next three years. OCU400, OCU410ST, and OCU410 remain on schedule for filings in 2026, 2027, and 2028, respectively.
  • OCU400, designed to treat retinitis pigmentosa (RP), demonstrated statistically significant safety and efficacy in a Phase 1/2 clinical trial. The Phase 3 trial has been initiated with plans for completion of enrollment in 2025, targeting a global patient population of 1.6 million.
  • Progress was reported for OCU410ST, addressing Stargardt disease, with the Phase 2/3 pivotal trial receiving alignment from FDA and EMA for its design. This trial is expected to begin mid-2025.
  • CFO Ramesh Ramachandran reported research and development expenses of $9.5M for the quarter, up from $6.8M in the same period last year. General and administrative expenses remained stable at $6.5M. The company ended the quarter with $38.1M in cash and restricted cash, supporting the runway into Q1 2026.

Outlook

  • Management confirmed that the manufacturing and process validations for OCU400 remain on track for completion in 2025, ensuring readiness for commercial launch in 2027.
  • For OCU410ST, the company plans to start the Phase 2/3 clinical trial by mid-2025, with data from this trial expected to support a BLA filing in 2027.
  • Interim results for the OCU410 Phase 2 clinical trial for geographic atrophy (GA) are expected in the fall of this year, with Phase 3 planned for initiation in 2026.
  • Management emphasized ongoing discussions with strategic partners and relevant government agencies to secure funding for vaccine and other program developments.

Financial Results

  • Net loss for the first quarter of 2025 was $15.3M, or ($0.05) per share, compared to a net loss of $11.9M, or ($0.05) per share, in the same quarter of 2024.
  • Cash and restricted cash decreased from $58.8M at the end of Q4 2024 to $38.1M as of Q1 2025, attributed to increased R&D investments.
  • The company reaffirmed that its current cash position will support operations through Q1 2026.

Q&A

  • Michael Okunewitch, Maxim Group, inquired about the liMeliGhT study for OCU400. Management confirmed enrollment is on track for completion in the first half of 2025.
  • Okunewitch asked about manufacturing readiness for OCU400’s commercialization. CEO Musunuri and CSO Arun Upadhyay detailed plans to complete process validations this year and leverage Malvern, Pennsylvania, facilities for U.S. production by 2027.
  • Swayampakula Ramakanth, H.C. Wainwright, questioned updates on all three gene therapy programs. Musunuri outlined timelines for interim data releases and Phase 3 trial initiations across programs, emphasizing confidence in achieving BLA targets.
  • Robert LeBoyer, Noble, requested clarity on European regulatory alignment for OCU410ST. Musunuri stated discussions with EMA are ongoing, but no additional trials are required for approval based on U.S. data.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed cautious optimism, frequently pressing for updates on timelines and funding assurances. Concerns over cash runway and competitive positioning in geographic atrophy treatments were notable.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing achievements and future milestones. Musunuri frequently highlighted the transformative potential of Ocugen’s therapies.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed consistent confidence but faced more detailed questions about regulatory and manufacturing readiness.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with a strong emphasis on maintaining timelines for BLA filings. However, there was increased focus on manufacturing readiness and regulatory engagement.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted slightly toward manufacturing and funding concerns, reflecting the company’s advancing clinical programs.
  • Financial metrics showed an increase in R&D expenses, aligning with Ocugen’s pipeline progress.
  • Management tone and confidence levels were steady, with consistent emphasis on the potential of the modifier gene therapy platform.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company highlighted the need for additional funding to support long-term clinical and commercial objectives. Management stated they are actively exploring strategic partnerships.
  • Analysts raised concerns about competitive pressures in the geographic atrophy market, given existing approved therapies.
  • Regulatory alignment with EMA for OCU410ST remains a pending matter, though management expressed optimism based on ongoing discussions.

Final Takeaway

Ocugen reinforced its commitment to advancing its modifier gene therapy platform, targeting multiple BLA filings over the next three years. The company is progressing clinical trials on schedule, with OCU400, OCU410ST, and OCU410 programs addressing significant unmet medical needs in retinitis pigmentosa, Stargardt disease, and geographic atrophy. Financially, the company is extending its cash runway while actively seeking partnerships to support its ambitious goals. Investors should monitor upcoming clinical data releases and regulatory developments for these programs.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Fiserv, Paysafe expand alliance to deliver enhanced SMB solutions
data.description:
Fiserv (NYSE:FI) expanded its long-term pact with Paysafe (NYSE:PSFE) to deliver several enhanced solutions for small and medium-sized businesses ("SMBs"), the companies said Friday.

The integration of Fiserv's Clover Capital tool, for instance, provides SMBs with improved access to capital to help them scale and grow. What's more, Paysafe (NYSE:PSFE) will leverage Fiserv's Data-as-a-Service solution to further enhance their risk and fraud protection and more effectively safeguard customers.

The two companies are also working on rolling out a digital wallet within Fiserv's (NYSE:FI) Clover merchant base, enabling SMBs to receive faster settlements and access a full range of banking services.

“Together, we are creating innovative, growth-oriented solutions that unlock new opportunities for SMBs while accelerating our product expansion strategies,” Paysafe CEO Bruce Lowthers said in a statement.

In Friday morning trading, Fiserv (FI), which during the week posted quarterly financial results, edged up 0.4%, while Paysafe (PSFE) gained 1.7%.

data.title: CrowdStrike dips after US probes role of execs in $32M Carahsoft deal - report
data.description:
U.S. prosecutors and regulators probing a $32 million deal between CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) and Carahsoft are investigating what senior company executives may have known about it and are reviewing other transactions done by CrowdStrike, Bloomberg News reported citing two people with knowledge of the matter.

Shares of CrowdStrike fell about 3% on Friday. 

In February it was reported that the U.S. Department of Justice and the Securities and Exchange Commission were investigating the deal to provide cybersecurity services for the Internal Revenue Service. The 2023 deal is under scrutiny because the IRS never bought the products, and investigators are looking if the transaction was properly accounted for.

CrowdStrike has previously said that Carahsoft made on time payments for the order, while Carahsoft previously said it stands by the transaction, the report added.

Now investigators have questioned former staff about how the deal was struck, what CrowdStrike’s leaders knew about it and if the staff raised concerns about other transactions, the report noted.

Investigators have also received internal CrowdStrike records, the report added.

The investigators’ questions indicate that the parallel SEC and DOJ investigations into CrowdStrike are broader than previously known, the report noted.

"As we have stated previously, we stand by the accounting of the transaction,” said CrowdStrike spokesperson Kevin Benacciin in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

Carahsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Around the time CrowdStrike closed the deal for the IRS, on the last day of a fiscal quarter in 2023, some employees at the cybersecurity provider raised concerns that it was “pre-booking” the transaction, Bloomberg previously reported. The staff viewed the transaction as incomplete because it was not clear if the IRS would ultimately buy the product.

In some cases, U.S. regulators have sued and fined companies over alleged pre-booking, also known as channel stuffing, claiming they misled investors by improperly recording revenue to inflate financial numbers, the report added.

In interviews starting last fall, prosecutors and regulators asked is CrowdStrike staff thought other deals were handled in similar ways. The investigators have specifically asked about another deal in 2023 involving the IRS that was worth over $1M, the report noted.

Probing officers also asked about multi-million dollar transactions for the Department of Health and Human Services and the Department of Energy, according to the report.

Among the internal records of  CrowdStrike obtained by investigators, are staff responses to questionnaires meant to ensure transactions adhere with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act. It is a law that was passed after several accounting scandals in the early 2000s aimed at reducing corporate fraud by improving financial auditing and public disclosure requirements.

One of the records showed an employee formally expressed concerns that the company did not handle the $32M transaction appropriately. Investigators are also seeking information about CrowdStrike’s process for closing the transaction and inquiring about who in the company’s sales and corporate leadership could have been involved in different aspects of it, the report stated.

The deal was big enough that it could have made a difference between CrowdStrike beating or missing Wall Street estimates on two key financial metrics for the quarter in which it closed in 2023, the report added.

A purchase order seen by Bloomberg divided the buy into four $8M payments, with the final payment due at the end of last October, the report noted.

Last November, CrowdStrike excluded about $26M from the annual recurring revenue in its quarterly earnings report. CFO Burt Podbere said the company decided that a transaction would not be repeated “after a distributor in the federal space provided notice of its intention to exercise transferability rights with respect to a transaction,” the report added.

data.title: Soho House GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.16, revenue of $282.86M misses by $1.61M
data.description:
• Soho House press release (NYSE:SHCO): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $282.86M (+8.0% Y/Y) misses by $1.61M.
  • Total Members grew 3.1% year-over-year to 269,636
  • Total Soho House Members grew 7.1% year-over-year to 212,001
  • Other Memberships including Soho Friends and Soho Works declined 9.3% year-over-year to 57,635 members.
  • Financing: SHCO ended first quarter 2025 with Cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash of $155 million.

data.title: LENZ Therapeutics inks exclusive commercialization agreement with Lotus Pharmaceutical
data.description:
• LENZ Therapeutics (NASDAQ:LENZ) and Lotus Pharmaceutical on Friday announced an exclusive license and commercialization agreement for Lotus to commercialize LNZ100 for the treatment of presbyopia in the Republic of Korea and certain countries in Southeast Asia.
  • Under the terms of the licensing and commercialization agreement, LENZ will receive up to $125M in upfront, regulatory and commercial milestone payments, as well as tiered, double-digit royalties on future net sales.
  • Lotus will have exclusive development, manufacturing, registration and commercialization rights for LNZ100 for the treatment of presbyopia in the Republic of Korea and certain countries in Southeast Asia, including Thailand, Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Brunei, Indonesia and Singapore.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Davide Campari-Milano reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Davide Campari-Milano press release (OTCPK:DVDCF): Q1 Net sales €666 million, down -4.2% organically and up +0.3% on a reported basis. The perimeter impact was +4.3% driven mainly by Courvoisier while the FX effect was +0.2%.
  • Group profit before taxation, at €107 million, -26.1% on a reported basis. Group profit before taxation-adjusted of €114 million, -22.5%.
